content: People рҹ‘Қрҹ‘Һрҹ‘Ҫрҹ‘јрҹ’ўрҹҳ рҹҳ§рҹҳІрҹ‘¶рҹ’ҷрҹҳҠрҹ’ҘрҹҷҮ:bowtie:рҹ‘Ұрҹ‘°рҹ’”рҹ‘Өрҹ‘Ҙрҹ‘Ҹрҹҳ°рҹ’Ҙрҹҳ–рҹҳ•рҹ‘·вҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹ‘®вҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹ‘«рҹ’‘рҹ’Ҹрҹҳўрҹҳҝрҹ’ҳрҹ’ғрҹ‘ҜвҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹ’ЁрҹҳһрҹҳҘрҹ’«рҹҳөрҹ’§рҹ‘Ӯвқ—рҹҳ‘рҹ‘Җрҹ‘Ҡрҹ‘ЁвҖҚрҹ‘©вҖҚрҹ‘ҰрҹҳЁ:feelsgood:рҹҗҫ:finnadie:рҹ”ҘвңҠрҹҳірҹҳҰрҹ–•рҹ‘§:goberserk::godmode:рҹ’ҡвқ•вқ”рҹҳ¬рҹҳҒрҹҳҖрҹ’ӮвҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹ’ҮвҖҚвҷҖпёҸвңӢрҹ’©рҹҷүвқӨпёҸрҹҳҚрҹҳ»рҹ’“рҹ’—:hurtrealbad:рҹҳҜрҹ‘ҝрҹ’ҒвҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹҳҮрҹ‘әрҹ‘№рҹҳӮрҹҳ№рҹ’Ӣрҹҳ—рҹҳҪрҹҳҡрҹҳҳрҹҳҷрҹҳҶрҹ‘„рҹ’Ңрҹ‘Ёрҹ‘Ірҹ‘івҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹҳ·рҹ’ҶвҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹӨҳрҹ’ӘрҹҺөрҹ’…:neckbeard:рҹҳҗрҹҷ…вҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹҳ¶рҹ‘ғрҹҺ¶рҹ‘ҢрҹҷҶвҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹ‘ҙрҹ‘өрҹ‘җрҹҳ®рҹҳ”рҹҳЈрҹҷҚвҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹ‘ұвҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹҷҺвҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹ‘Үрҹ‘Ҳрҹ‘үвҳқпёҸрҹ‘Ҷрҹ’©рҹҳҫрҹҷҸрҹ‘ёрҹ‘Ҡрҹ’ңвқ“рҹҳЎ:rage1::rage2::rage3::rage4:вңӢрҹҷҢрҹҷӢвҖҚвҷҖпёҸвҳәпёҸрҹҳҢрҹ’һрҹҸғвҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹҸғрҹҳҶрҹҳұрҹҷҖрҹҷҲрҹ’©рҹ’ҖрҹҳҙрҹҳӘрҹҳ„рҹҳёрҹҳғрҹҳәрҹҳҲрҹҳҸрҹҳјрҹҳӯвңЁрҹ’–рҹҷҠрҹ’¬вӯҗрҹҢҹрҹҳӣрҹҳқрҹҳңрҹҳҺ:suspect:рҹҳ“рҹ’Ұрҹҳ…рҹ’ӯрҹ‘Һрҹ‘Қрҹҳ«рҹ‘…рҹҳӨ:trollface:рҹ’•рҹ‘¬рҹ‘ӯрҹҳ’вңҢпёҸрҹҡ¶вҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹ‘Ӣрҹҳ©рҹҳүрҹ‘©рҹҳҹрҹ’ӣрҹҳӢрҹ’Ө

content: Nature рҹҗңрҹҗӨрҹҗ»рҹҗқрҹӘІрҹҗҰрҹҢјрҹҗЎрҹҗ—рҹ’җрҹҗӣрҹҢөрҹҗ«рҹҗұрҹҗҲрҹҢёрҹҢ°рҹҗ”вҳҒпёҸрҹҗ®рҹҗ„рҹҢҷрҹҗҠрҹҢҖрҹҢірҹҗ¶рҹҗ•рҹҗ¬рҹҗүрҹҗІрҹҗӘрҹҢҫрҹҢҚрҹҢҺрҹҢҸрҹҗҳрҹҢІрҹҚӮрҹҢ“рҹҢӣрҹҗҹрҹҢҒрҹҚҖрҹҗёрҹҢ•рҹҢқрҹҢҗрҹҗҗрҹҗ№рҹҗҘрҹҗЈрҹҢҝрҹҢәрҹҗқрҹҗҙрҹҗЁрҹҢ—рҹҢңрҹҚғрҹҗҶрҹҚҒрҹҢҢрҹҗ’рҹҗөрҹҢ”рҹҗӯрҹҗҒрҹҚ„рҹҢ‘рҹҢҡрҹҢғрҹҢҠ:octocat:рҹҗҷрҹҗӮрҹҢҙрҹҗјвӣ…рҹҗҫрҹҗ§рҹҗ·рҹҗ–рҹҗҪрҹҗ©рҹҗ°рҹҗҮрҹҗҺрҹҗҸрҹҗҖрҹҗ“рҹҢ№рҹҢұрҹҗ‘рҹҗҡрҹҗҢрҹҗҚвқ„пёҸвҳғпёҸ:squirrel:рҹҢһрҹҢ»вҳҖпёҸрҹҗҜрҹҗ…рҹҗ рҹҢ·рҹҗўвҳӮпёҸрҹҢӢрҹҢҳрҹҢ–рҹҗғрҹҢ’рҹҢ”рҹҗірҹҗӢрҹҗәвҡЎ

content: People рҹ‘Қрҹ‘Һрҹ‘Ҫрҹ‘јрҹ’ўрҹҳ рҹҳ§рҹҳІрҹ‘¶рҹ’ҷрҹҳҠрҹ’ҘрҹҷҮ:bowtie:рҹ‘Ұрҹ‘°рҹ’”рҹ‘Өрҹ‘Ҙрҹ‘Ҹрҹҳ°рҹ’Ҙрҹҳ–рҹҳ•рҹ‘·вҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹ‘®вҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹ‘«рҹ’‘рҹ’Ҹрҹҳўрҹҳҝрҹ’ҳрҹ’ғрҹ‘ҜвҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹ’ЁрҹҳһрҹҳҘрҹ’«рҹҳөрҹ’§рҹ‘Ӯвқ—рҹҳ‘рҹ‘Җрҹ‘Ҡрҹ‘ЁвҖҚрҹ‘©вҖҚрҹ‘ҰрҹҳЁ:feelsgood:рҹҗҫ:finnadie:рҹ”ҘвңҠрҹҳірҹҳҰрҹ–•рҹ‘§:goberserk::godmode:рҹ’ҡвқ•вқ”рҹҳ¬рҹҳҒрҹҳҖрҹ’ӮвҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹ’ҮвҖҚвҷҖпёҸвңӢрҹ’©рҹҷүвқӨпёҸрҹҳҚрҹҳ»рҹ’“рҹ’—:hurtrealbad:рҹҳҜрҹ‘ҝрҹ’ҒвҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹҳҮрҹ‘әрҹ‘№рҹҳӮрҹҳ№рҹ’Ӣрҹҳ—рҹҳҪрҹҳҡрҹҳҳрҹҳҷрҹҳҶрҹ‘„рҹ’Ңрҹ‘Ёрҹ‘Ірҹ‘івҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹҳ·рҹ’ҶвҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹӨҳрҹ’ӘрҹҺөрҹ’…:neckbeard:рҹҳҗрҹҷ…вҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹҳ¶рҹ‘ғрҹҺ¶рҹ‘ҢрҹҷҶвҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹ‘ҙрҹ‘өрҹ‘җрҹҳ®рҹҳ”рҹҳЈрҹҷҚвҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹ‘ұвҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹҷҺвҖҚвҷҖпёҸрҹ‘Үрҹ‘Ҳрҹ‘үвҳқпёҸрҹ‘Ҷрҹ’©рҹҳҫрҹҷҸрҹ‘ёрҹ‘Ҡрҹ’ңвқ“рҹҳЎ:rage1::rage2::rage3::rage4:вңӢрҹҷҢрҹҷӢвҖҚвҷҖпёҸвҳәпёҸрҹҳҢрҹ’һрҹҸғвҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹҸғрҹҳҶрҹҳұрҹҷҖрҹҷҲрҹ’©рҹ’ҖрҹҳҙрҹҳӘрҹҳ„рҹҳёрҹҳғрҹҳәрҹҳҲрҹҳҸрҹҳјрҹҳӯвңЁрҹ’–рҹҷҠрҹ’¬вӯҗрҹҢҹрҹҳӣрҹҳқрҹҳңрҹҳҺ:suspect:рҹҳ“рҹ’Ұрҹҳ…рҹ’ӯрҹ‘Һрҹ‘Қрҹҳ«рҹ‘…рҹҳӨ:trollface:рҹ’•рҹ‘¬рҹ‘ӯрҹҳ’вңҢпёҸрҹҡ¶вҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹ‘Ӣрҹҳ©рҹҳүрҹ‘©рҹҳҹрҹ’ӣрҹҳӢрҹ’Ө

content: Nature рҹҗңрҹҗӨрҹҗ»рҹҗқрҹӘІрҹҗҰрҹҢјрҹҗЎрҹҗ—рҹ’җрҹҗӣрҹҢөрҹҗ«рҹҗұрҹҗҲрҹҢёрҹҢ°рҹҗ”вҳҒпёҸрҹҗ®рҹҗ„рҹҢҷрҹҗҠрҹҢҖрҹҢірҹҗ¶рҹҗ•рҹҗ¬рҹҗүрҹҗІрҹҗӘрҹҢҫрҹҢҚрҹҢҺрҹҢҸрҹҗҳрҹҢІрҹҚӮрҹҢ“рҹҢӣрҹҗҹрҹҢҒрҹҚҖрҹҗёрҹҢ•рҹҢқрҹҢҗрҹҗҗрҹҗ№рҹҗҘрҹҗЈрҹҢҝрҹҢәрҹҗқрҹҗҙрҹҗЁрҹҢ—рҹҢңрҹҚғрҹҗҶрҹҚҒрҹҢҢрҹҗ’рҹҗөрҹҢ”рҹҗӯрҹҗҒрҹҚ„рҹҢ‘рҹҢҡрҹҢғрҹҢҠ:octocat:рҹҗҷрҹҗӮрҹҢҙрҹҗјвӣ…рҹҗҫрҹҗ§рҹҗ·рҹҗ–рҹҗҪрҹҗ©рҹҗ°рҹҗҮрҹҗҺрҹҗҸрҹҗҖрҹҗ“рҹҢ№рҹҢұрҹҗ‘рҹҗҡрҹҗҢрҹҗҚвқ„пёҸвҳғпёҸ:squirrel:рҹҢһрҹҢ»вҳҖпёҸрҹҗҜрҹҗ…рҹҗ рҹҢ·рҹҗўвҳӮпёҸрҹҢӢрҹҢҳрҹҢ–рҹҗғрҹҢ’рҹҢ”рҹҗірҹҗӢрҹҗәвҡЎ

content: Objects рҹҺұвҸ°рҹҚҺрҹҺЁрҹ‘ҹрҹҚјрҹҺҲрҹҺҚрҹҚҢрҹ“ҠвҡҫрҹҸҖрҹӣҖрҹӣҒрҹ”ӢрҹҚәрҹҚ»рҹ””рҹҚұрҹҡҙвҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹ‘ҷрҹҺӮрҹғҸвң’пёҸрҹ“ҳрҹ’Јрҹ“–рҹ”–рҹ“‘рҹ“ҡрҹ‘ўрҹҺірҹҚһрҹ’јрҹ’ЎрҹҚ°рҹ“Ҷрҹ“Ірҹ“·рҹҚ¬рҹ“Үрҹ’ҝрҹ“үрҹ“ҲрҹҚ’рҹҚ«рҹҺ„рҹҺ¬рҹ“Ӣрҹ“•рҹ”җрҹҢӮвҷЈпёҸрҹҚёвҳ•рҹ’»рҹҺҠрҹҚӘрҹҢҪрҹ’ірҹ‘‘рҹ”®рҹҚӣрҹҚ®рҹҚЎрҹҺҜрҹ“…вҷҰпёҸрҹ’өрҹҺҺрҹҡӘрҹҚ©рҹ‘—рҹ“Җрҹ“§рҹҘҡрҹҚҶрҹ”ҢвңүпёҸвңүрҹ“©рҹ’¶рҹ‘“рҹ“ рҹ“ҒрҹҺҶрҹҚҘрҹҺЈрҹҺҸрҹ”Ұрҹҗ¬рҹ’ҫрҹҺҙрҹҸҲрҹ‘ЈрҹҚҙрҹҚӨрҹҚҹрҹҺІрҹ’Һрҹ‘»рҹҺҒрҹ’қвӣірҹҚҮрҹҚҸрҹ“—рҹҺёрҹ”«рҹҚ”рҹ”Ёрҹ‘ңрҹҺ§вҷҘпёҸрҹ”Ҷрҹ‘ рҹ”ӘрҹҚҜрҹҸҮвҢӣвҸірҹҚЁрҹҚҰрҹ“Ҙрҹ“Ёрҹ“ұрҹҺғрҹ‘–рҹ”‘рҹ‘ҳрҹҸ®рҹ“’рҹҚӢрҹ’„рҹ”’рҹ”ҸрҹҚӯвһҝрҹ”Ҡрҹ“ўрҹ”…рҹ”Қрҹ”ҺрҹҖ„рҹ“«рҹ“Әрҹ“¬рҹ“ӯрҹ‘һрҹҚ–рҹ“ЈрҹҚҲрҹ“қрҹҺӨрҹ”¬рҹ’Ҫрҹ’ёрҹ’°рҹҺ“рҹҡөвҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹҺҘрҹҺ№рҹҺјрҹ”Үрҹ“ӣрҹ‘”рҹ“°рҹ”•рҹ““рҹ“”рҹ”©рҹҚўрҹ“–рҹ“Ӯрҹ“ҷрҹ“Өрҹ“Ұрҹ“„рҹ“ғрҹ“ҹрҹ“ҺрҹҚ‘рҹҚҗвңҸвңҸпёҸвҳҺпёҸрҹ’ҠрҹҚҚрҹҚ•рҹ“Ҝрҹ“®рҹ‘қрҹҚ—рҹ’·рҹ‘ӣрҹ“Ңрҹ“»рҹҚңрҹҺҖрҹҚҡрҹҚҷрҹҚҳрҹҺ‘рҹ’ҚрҹҸүрҹҺҪрҹҚ¶рҹ‘ЎрҹҺ…рҹӣ°пёҸрҹҺ·рҹҺ’вңӮпёҸрҹ“ңрҹ’әрҹҚ§рҹ‘•рҹ‘һрҹҡҝрҹҺҝрҹҡ¬рҹҸӮвҡҪрҹ”үрҹ‘ҫвҷ пёҸрҹҚқвқҮпёҸрҹҺҮрҹ”ҲрҹҚІрҹ“ҸрҹҚ“рҹҸ„вҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹҚЈрҹҚ рҹҸҠвҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹ’үрҹҺүрҹҺӢрҹҚҠрҹҚөвҳҺрҹ“һрҹ”ӯрҹҺҫрҹҡҪрҹҚ…рҹҺ©рҹ“җрҹҸҶрҹҚ№рҹҺәрҹ‘•рҹ“әрҹ”“рҹ“јрҹ“№рҹҺ®рҹҺ»вҢҡрҹҚүрҹҺҗрҹҚ·рҹ‘ҡрҹ‘’рҹ”§рҹ’ҙ

content: Introduction This tutorial will show you how to create a simple theme in Hugo. I assume that you are familiar with HTML, the bash command line, and that you are comfortable using Markdown to format content. IвҖҷll explain how Hugo uses templates and how you can organize your templates to create a theme. I wonвҖҷt cover using CSS to style your theme. WeвҖҷll start with creating a new site with a very basic template. Then weвҖҷll add in a few pages and posts. With small variations on that, you will be able to create many different types of web sites. In this tutorial, commands that you enter will start with the вҖң$вҖқ prompt. The output will follow. Lines that start with вҖң#вҖқ are comments that IвҖҷve added to explain a point. When I show updates to a file, the вҖң:wqвҖқ on the last line means to save the file. HereвҖҷs an example: ## this is a comment $ echo this is a command this is a command ## edit the file $vi foo.md +++ date = "2014-09-28" title = "creating a new theme" +++ bah and humbug :wq ## show it $ cat foo.md +++ date = "2014-09-28" title = "creating a new theme" +++ bah and humbug $
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Some Definitions There are a few concepts that you need to understand before creating a theme.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Skins Skins are the files responsible for the look and feel of your site. ItвҖҷs the CSS that controls colors and fonts, itвҖҷs the Javascript that determines actions and reactions. ItвҖҷs also the rules that Hugo uses to transform your content into the HTML that the site will serve to visitors. You have two ways to create a skin. The simplest way is to create it in the layouts/ directory. If you do, then you donвҖҷt have to worry about configuring Hugo to recognize it. The first place that Hugo will look for rules and files is in the layouts/ directory so it will always find the skin. Your second choice is to create it in a sub-directory of the themes/ directory. If you do, then you must always tell Hugo where to search for the skin. ItвҖҷs extra work, though, so why bother with it? The difference between creating a skin in layouts/ and creating it in themes/ is very subtle. A skin in layouts/ canвҖҷt be customized without updating the templates and static files that it is built from. A skin created in themes/, on the other hand, can be and that makes it easier for other people to use it. The rest of this tutorial will call a skin created in the themes/ directory a theme. Note that you can use this tutorial to create a skin in the layouts/ directory if you wish to. The main difference will be that you wonвҖҷt need to update the siteвҖҷs configuration file to use a theme.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: The Home Page The home page, or landing page, is the first page that many visitors to a site see. It is the index.html file in the root directory of the web site. Since Hugo writes files to the public/ directory, our home page is public/index.html.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Site Configuration File When Hugo runs, it looks for a configuration file that contains settings that override default values for the entire site. The file can use TOML, YAML, or JSON. I prefer to use TOML for my configuration files. If you prefer to use JSON or YAML, youвҖҷll need to translate my examples. YouвҖҷll also need to change the name of the file since Hugo uses the extension to determine how to process it. Hugo translates Markdown files into HTML. By default, Hugo expects to find Markdown files in your content/ directory and template files in your themes/ directory. It will create HTML files in your public/ directory. You can change this by specifying alternate locations in the configuration file.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Content Content is stored in text files that contain two sections. The first section is the вҖңfront matter,вҖқ which is the meta-information on the content. The second section contains Markdown that will be converted to HTML. Front Matter The front matter is information about the content. Like the configuration file, it can be written in TOML, YAML, or JSON. Unlike the configuration file, Hugo doesnвҖҷt use the fileвҖҷs extension to know the format. It looks for markers to signal the type. TOML is surrounded by вҖң+++вҖқ, YAML by вҖң---вҖқ, and JSON is enclosed in curly braces. I prefer to use TOML, so youвҖҷll need to translate my examples if you prefer YAML or JSON. The information in the front matter is passed into the template before the content is rendered into HTML. Markdown Content is written in Markdown which makes it easier to create the content. Hugo runs the content through a Markdown engine to create the HTML which will be written to the output file.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Template Files Hugo uses template files to render content into HTML. Template files are a bridge between the content and presentation. Rules in the template define what content is published, where itвҖҷs published to, and how it will rendered to the HTML file. The template guides the presentation by specifying the style to use. There are three types of templates: single, list, and partial. Each type takes a bit of content as input and transforms it based on the commands in the template. Hugo uses its knowledge of the content to find the template file used to render the content. If it canвҖҷt find a template that is an exact match for the content, it will shift up a level and search from there. It will continue to do so until it finds a matching template or runs out of templates to try. If it canвҖҷt find a template, it will use the default template for the site. Please note that you can use the front matter to influence HugoвҖҷs choice of templates. Single Template A single template is used to render a single piece of content. For example, an article or post would be a single piece of content and use a single template. List Template A list template renders a group of related content. That could be a summary of recent postings or all articles in a category. List templates can contain multiple groups. The homepage template is a special type of list template. Hugo assumes that the home page of your site will act as the portal for the rest of the content in the site. Partial Template A partial template is a template that can be included in other templates. Partial templates must be called using the вҖңpartialвҖқ template command. They are very handy for rolling up common behavior. For example, your site may have a banner that all pages use. Instead of copying the text of the banner into every single and list template, you could create a partial with the banner in it. That way if you decide to change the banner, you only have to change the partial template.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Create a New Site LetвҖҷs use Hugo to create a new web site. IвҖҷm a Mac user, so IвҖҷll create mine in my home directory, in the Sites folder. If youвҖҷre using Linux, you might have to create the folder first. The вҖңnew siteвҖқ command will create a skeleton of a site. It will give you the basic directory structure and a useable configuration file. $ hugo new site ~/Sites/zafta $ cd ~/Sites/zafta $ ls -l total 8 drwxr-xr-x 7 quoha staff 238 Sep 29 16:49 . drwxr-xr-x 3 quoha staff 102 Sep 29 16:49 .. drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 archetypes -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 82 Sep 29 16:49 config.toml drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 content drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 layouts drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 static $ Take a look in the content/ directory to confirm that it is empty. The other directories (archetypes/, layouts/, and static/) are used when customizing a theme. ThatвҖҷs a topic for a different tutorial, so please ignore them for now.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Generate the HTML For the New Site Running the hugo command with no options will read all the available content and generate the HTML files. It will also copy all static files (thatвҖҷs everything thatвҖҷs not content). Since we have an empty site, it wonвҖҷt do much, but it will do it very quickly. $ hugo --verbose INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [index.html _default/list.html _default/single.html] WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 0 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 2 ms $ The вҖң--verboseвҖқ flag gives extra information that will be helpful when we build the template. Every line of the output that starts with вҖңINFO:вҖқ or вҖңWARN:вҖқ is present because we used that flag. The lines that start with вҖңWARN:вҖқ are warning messages. WeвҖҷll go over them later. We can verify that the command worked by looking at the directory again. $ ls -l total 8 drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 archetypes -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 82 Sep 29 16:49 config.toml drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 content drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 layouts drwxr-xr-x 4 quoha staff 136 Sep 29 17:02 public drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 static $ See that new public/ directory? Hugo placed all generated content there. When youвҖҷre ready to publish your web site, thatвҖҷs the place to start. For now, though, letвҖҷs just confirm that we have what weвҖҷd expect from a site with no content. $ ls -l public total 16 -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 416 Sep 29 17:02 index.xml -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 262 Sep 29 17:02 sitemap.xml $ Hugo created two XML files, which is standard, but there are no HTML files.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Test the New Site Verify that you can run the built-in web server. It will dramatically shorten your development cycle if you do. Start it by running the вҖңserverвҖқ command. If it is successful, you will see output similar to the following: $ hugo server --verbose INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [index.html _default/list.html _default/single.html] WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 0 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 2 ms Serving pages from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public Web Server is available at http://localhost:1313 Press Ctrl+C to stop Connect to the listed URL (itвҖҷs on the line that starts with вҖңWeb ServerвҖқ). If everything is working correctly, you should get a page that shows the following: index.xml sitemap.xml ThatвҖҷs a listing of your public/ directory. Hugo didnвҖҷt create a home page because our site has no content. When thereвҖҷs no index.html file in a directory, the server lists the files in the directory, which is what you should see in your browser. LetвҖҷs go back and look at those warnings again. WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [index.html _default/list.html _default/single.html] WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html] That second warning is easier to explain. We havenвҖҷt created a template to be used to generate вҖңpage not found errors.вҖқ The 404 message is a topic for a separate tutorial. Now for the first warning. It is for the home page. You can tell because the first layout that it looked for was вҖңindex.html.вҖқ ThatвҖҷs only used by the home page. I like that the verbose flag causes Hugo to list the files that itвҖҷs searching for. For the home page, they are index.html, _default/list.html, and _default/single.html. There are some rules that weвҖҷll cover later that explain the names and paths. For now, just remember that Hugo couldnвҖҷt find a template for the home page and it told you so. At this point, youвҖҷve got a working installation and site that we can build upon. All thatвҖҷs left is to add some content and a theme to display it.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Create a New Theme Hugo doesnвҖҷt ship with a default theme. There are a few available (I counted a dozen when I first installed Hugo) and Hugo comes with a command to create new themes. WeвҖҷre going to create a new theme called вҖңzafta.вҖқ Since the goal of this tutorial is to show you how to fill out the files to pull in your content, the theme will not contain any CSS. In other words, ugly but functional. All themes have opinions on content and layout. For example, Zafta uses вҖңpostвҖқ over вҖңblogвҖқ. Strong opinions make for simpler templates but differing opinions make it tougher to use themes. When you build a theme, consider using the terms that other themes do.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Create a Skeleton Use the hugo вҖңnewвҖқ command to create the skeleton of a theme. This creates the directory structure and places empty files for you to fill out. $ hugo new theme zafta $ ls -l total 8 drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 archetypes -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 82 Sep 29 16:49 config.toml drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 content drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 layouts drwxr-xr-x 4 quoha staff 136 Sep 29 17:02 public drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 static drwxr-xr-x 3 quoha staff 102 Sep 29 17:31 themes $ find themes -type f | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 1081 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/LICENSE.md -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/archetypes/default.md -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/_default/list.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/_default/single.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/partials/footer.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/partials/header.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 93 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/theme.toml $ The skeleton includes templates (the files ending in .html), license file, a description of your theme (the theme.toml file), and an empty archetype. Please take a minute to fill out the theme.toml and LICENSE.md files. TheyвҖҷre optional, but if youвҖҷre going to be distributing your theme, it tells the world who to praise (or blame). ItвҖҷs also nice to declare the license so that people will know how they can use the theme. $ vi themes/zafta/theme.toml author = "michael d henderson" description = "a minimal working template" license = "MIT" name = "zafta" source_repo = "" tags = ["tags", "categories"] :wq ## also edit themes/zafta/LICENSE.md and change ## the bit that says "YOUR_NAME_HERE" Note that the the skeletonвҖҷs template files are empty. DonвҖҷt worry, weвҖҷll be changing that shortly. $ find themes/zafta -name '*.html' | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/_default/list.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/_default/single.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/partials/footer.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/partials/header.html $
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Update the Configuration File to Use the Theme Now that weвҖҷve got a theme to work with, itвҖҷs a good idea to add the theme name to the configuration file. This is optional, because you can always add вҖң-t zaftaвҖқ on all your commands. I like to put it the configuration file because I like shorter command lines. If you donвҖҷt put it in the configuration file or specify it on the command line, you wonвҖҷt use the template that youвҖҷre expecting to. Edit the file to add the theme, add a title for the site, and specify that all of our content will use the TOML format. $ vi config.toml theme = "zafta" baseurl = "" languageCode = "en-us" title = "zafta - totally refreshing" MetaDataFormat = "toml" :wq $
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Generate the Site Now that we have an empty theme, letвҖҷs generate the site again. $ hugo --verbose INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html theme/404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 0 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 2 ms $ Did you notice that the output is different? The warning message for the home page has disappeared and we have an additional information line saying that Hugo is syncing from the themeвҖҷs directory. LetвҖҷs check the public/ directory to see what HugoвҖҷs created. $ ls -l public total 16 drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 17:56 css -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:56 index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 407 Sep 29 17:56 index.xml drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 17:56 js -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 243 Sep 29 17:56 sitemap.xml $ Notice four things: Hugo created a home page. This is the file public/index.html. Hugo created a css/ directory. Hugo created a js/ directory. Hugo claimed that it created 0 pages. It created a file and copied over static files, but didnвҖҷt create any pages. ThatвҖҷs because it considers a вҖңpageвҖқ to be a file created directly from a content file. It doesnвҖҷt count things like the index.html files that it creates automatically. The Home Page Hugo supports many different types of templates. The home page is special because it gets its own type of template and its own template file. The file, layouts/index.html, is used to generate the HTML for the home page. The Hugo documentation says that this is the only required template, but that depends. HugoвҖҷs warning message shows that it looks for three different templates: WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [index.html _default/list.html _default/single.html] If it canвҖҷt find any of these, it completely skips creating the home page. We noticed that when we built the site without having a theme installed. When Hugo created our theme, it created an empty home page template. Now, when we build the site, Hugo finds the template and uses it to generate the HTML for the home page. Since the template file is empty, the HTML file is empty, too. If the template had any rules in it, then Hugo would have used them to generate the home page. $ find . -name index.html | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 20:21 ./public/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 ./themes/zafta/layouts/index.html $ The Magic of Static Hugo does two things when generating the site. It uses templates to transform content into HTML and it copies static files into the site. Unlike content, static files are not transformed. They are copied exactly as they are. Hugo assumes that your site will use both CSS and JavaScript, so it creates directories in your theme to hold them. Remember opinions? Well, HugoвҖҷs opinion is that youвҖҷll store your CSS in a directory named css/ and your JavaScript in a directory named js/. If you donвҖҷt like that, you can change the directory names in your theme directory or even delete them completely. HugoвҖҷs nice enough to offer its opinion, then behave nicely if you disagree. $ find themes/zafta -type d | xargs ls -ld drwxr-xr-x 7 quoha staff 238 Sep 29 17:38 themes/zafta drwxr-xr-x 3 quoha staff 102 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/archetypes drwxr-xr-x 5 quoha staff 170 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts drwxr-xr-x 4 quoha staff 136 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/_default drwxr-xr-x 4 quoha staff 136 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/partials drwxr-xr-x 4 quoha staff 136 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/static drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/static/css drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/static/js $
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: The Theme Development Cycle When youвҖҷre working on a theme, you will make changes in the themeвҖҷs directory, rebuild the site, and check your changes in the browser. Hugo makes this very easy: Purge the public/ directory. Run the built in web server in watch mode. Open your site in a browser. Update the theme. Glance at your browser window to see changes. Return to step 4. IвҖҷll throw in one more opinion: never work on a theme on a live site. Always work on a copy of your site. Make changes to your theme, test them, then copy them up to your site. For added safety, use a tool like Git to keep a revision history of your content and your theme. Believe me when I say that it is too easy to lose both your mind and your changes. Check the main Hugo site for information on using Git with Hugo.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Purge the public/ Directory When generating the site, Hugo will create new files and update existing ones in the public/ directory. It will not delete files that are no longer used. For example, files that were created in the wrong directory or with the wrong title will remain. If you leave them, you might get confused by them later. I recommend cleaning out your site prior to generating it. Note: If youвҖҷre building on an SSD, you should ignore this. Churning on a SSD can be costly.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: HugoвҖҷs Watch Option HugoвҖҷs вҖң--watchвҖқ option will monitor the content/ and your theme directories for changes and rebuild the site automatically.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Live Reload HugoвҖҷs built in web server supports live reload. As pages are saved on the server, the browser is told to refresh the page. Usually, this happens faster than you can say, вҖңWow, thatвҖҷs totally amazing.вҖқ
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Development Commands Use the following commands as the basis for your workflow. ## purge old files. hugo will recreate the public directory. ## $ rm -rf public ## ## run hugo in watch mode ## $ hugo server --watch --verbose HereвҖҷs sample output showing Hugo detecting a change to the template for the home page. Once generated, the web browser automatically reloaded the page. IвҖҷve said this before, itвҖҷs amazing. $ rm -rf public $ hugo server --watch --verbose INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html theme/404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 0 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 2 ms Watching for changes in /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/content Serving pages from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public Web Server is available at http://localhost:1313 Press Ctrl+C to stop INFO: 2014/09/29 File System Event: ["/Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/layouts/index.html": MODIFY|ATTRIB] Change detected, rebuilding site WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html theme/404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 0 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 1 ms
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Update the Home Page Template The home page is one of a few special pages that Hugo creates automatically. As mentioned earlier, it looks for one of three files in the themeвҖҷs layout/ directory: index.html _default/list.html _default/single.html We could update one of the default templates, but a good design decision is to update the most specific template available. ThatвҖҷs not a hard and fast rule (in fact, weвҖҷll break it a few times in this tutorial), but it is a good generalization.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content:
Make a Static Home Page Right now, that page is empty because we donвҖҷt have any content and we donвҖҷt have any logic in the template. LetвҖҷs change that by adding some text to the template. $ vi themes/zafta/layouts/index.html

hugo says hello!

:wq $ Build the web site and then verify the results. $ hugo --verbose INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html theme/404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 0 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 2 ms $ find public -type f -name '*.html' | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 78 Sep 29 21:26 public/index.html $ cat public/index.html

hugo says hello!

Live Reload Note: If youвҖҷre running the server with the --watch option, youвҖҷll see different content in the file: $ cat public/index.html

hugo says hello!

When you use --watch, the Live Reload script is added by Hugo. Look for live reload in the documentation to see what it does and how to disable it.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Build a вҖңDynamicвҖқ Home Page вҖңDynamic home page?вҖқ HugoвҖҷs a static web site generator, so this seems an odd thing to say. I mean letвҖҷs have the home page automatically reflect the content in the site every time Hugo builds it. WeвҖҷll use iteration in the template to do that. Create New Posts Now that we have the home page generating static content, letвҖҷs add some content to the site. WeвҖҷll display these posts as a list on the home page and on their own page, too. Hugo has a command to generate a skeleton post, just like it does for sites and themes. $ hugo --verbose new post/first.md INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 attempting to create post/first.md of post INFO: 2014/09/29 curpath: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/archetypes/default.md ERROR: 2014/09/29 Unable to Cast <nil> to map[string]interface{} $ That wasnвҖҷt very nice, was it? The вҖңnewвҖқ command uses an archetype to create the post file. Hugo created an empty default archetype file, but that causes an error when thereвҖҷs a theme. For me, the workaround was to create an archetypes file specifically for the post type. $ vi themes/zafta/archetypes/post.md +++ Description = "" Tags = [] Categories = [] +++ :wq $ find themes/zafta/archetypes -type f | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 21:53 themes/zafta/archetypes/default.md -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 51 Sep 29 21:54 themes/zafta/archetypes/post.md $ hugo --verbose new post/first.md INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 attempting to create post/first.md of post INFO: 2014/09/29 curpath: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/archetypes/post.md INFO: 2014/09/29 creating /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/content/post/first.md /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/content/post/first.md created $ hugo --verbose new post/second.md INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 attempting to create post/second.md of post INFO: 2014/09/29 curpath: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/archetypes/post.md INFO: 2014/09/29 creating /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/content/post/second.md /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/content/post/second.md created $ ls -l content/post total 16 -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 104 Sep 29 21:54 first.md -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 105 Sep 29 21:57 second.md $ cat content/post/first.md +++ Categories = [] Description = "" Tags = [] date = "2014-09-29T21:54:53-05:00" title = "first" +++ my first post $ cat content/post/second.md +++ Categories = [] Description = "" Tags = [] date = "2014-09-29T21:57:09-05:00" title = "second" +++ my second post $ Build the web site and then verify the results. $ rm -rf public $ hugo --verbose INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 found taxonomies: map[string]string{"category":"categories", "tag":"tags"} WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html theme/404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 2 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 4 ms $ The output says that it created 2 pages. Those are our new posts: $ find public -type f -name '*.html' | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 78 Sep 29 22:13 public/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 22:13 public/post/first/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 22:13 public/post/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 22:13 public/post/second/index.html $ The new files are empty because because the templates used to generate the content are empty. The homepage doesnвҖҷt show the new content, either. We have to update the templates to add the posts.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: List and Single Templates In Hugo, we have three major kinds of templates. ThereвҖҷs the home page template that we updated previously. It is used only by the home page. We also have вҖңsingleвҖқ templates which are used to generate output for a single content file. We also have вҖңlistвҖқ templates that are used to group multiple pieces of content before generating output. Generally speaking, list templates are named вҖңlist.htmlвҖқ and single templates are named вҖңsingle.html.вҖқ There are three other types of templates: partials, content views, and terms. We will not go into much detail on these.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content:
Add Content to the Homepage The home page will contain a list of posts. LetвҖҷs update its template to add the posts that we just created. The logic in the template will run every time we build the site. $ vi themes/zafta/layouts/index.html {{ range first 10 .Data.Pages }}

{{ .Title }}

{{ end }} :wq $ Hugo uses the Go template engine. That engine scans the template files for commands which are enclosed between вҖң{{вҖқ and вҖң}}вҖқ. In our template, the commands are: range .Title end The вҖңrangeвҖқ command is an iterator. WeвҖҷre going to use it to go through the first ten pages. Every HTML file that Hugo creates is treated as a page, so looping through the list of pages will look at every file that will be created. The вҖң.TitleвҖқ command prints the value of the вҖңtitleвҖқ variable. Hugo pulls it from the front matter in the Markdown file. The вҖңendвҖқ command signals the end of the range iterator. The engine loops back to the top of the iteration when it finds вҖңend.вҖқ Everything between the вҖңrangeвҖқ and вҖңendвҖқ is evaluated every time the engine goes through the iteration. In this file, that would cause the title from the first ten pages to be output as heading level one. ItвҖҷs helpful to remember that some variables, like .Data, are created before any output files. Hugo loads every content file into the variable and then gives the template a chance to process before creating the HTML files. Build the web site and then verify the results. $ rm -rf public $ hugo --verbose INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 found taxonomies: map[string]string{"tag":"tags", "category":"categories"} WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html theme/404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 2 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 4 ms $ find public -type f -name '*.html' | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 94 Sep 29 22:23 public/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 22:23 public/post/first/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 22:23 public/post/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 22:23 public/post/second/index.html $ cat public/index.html

second

first

$ Congratulations, the home page shows the title of the two posts. The posts themselves are still empty, but letвҖҷs take a moment to appreciate what weвҖҷve done. Your template now generates output dynamically. Believe it or not, by inserting the range command inside of those curly braces, youвҖҷve learned everything you need to know to build a theme. All thatвҖҷs really left is understanding which template will be used to generate each content file and becoming familiar with the commands for the template engine. And, if that were entirely true, this tutorial would be much shorter. There are a few things to know that will make creating a new template much easier. DonвҖҷt worry, though, thatвҖҷs all to come.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content:
Add Content to the Posts WeвҖҷre working with posts, which are in the content/post/ directory. That means that their section is вҖңpostвҖқ (and if we donвҖҷt do something weird, their type is also вҖңpostвҖқ). Hugo uses the section and type to find the template file for every piece of content. Hugo will first look for a template file that matches the section or type name. If it canвҖҷt find one, then it will look in the _default/ directory. There are some twists that weвҖҷll cover when we get to categories and tags, but for now we can assume that Hugo will try post/single.html, then _default/single.html. Now that we know the search rule, letвҖҷs see what we actually have available: $ find themes/zafta -name single.html | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 132 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/_default/single.html We could create a new template, post/single.html, or change the default. Since we donвҖҷt know of any other content types, letвҖҷs start with updating the default. Remember, any content that we havenвҖҷt created a template for will end up using this template. That can be good or bad. Bad because I know that weвҖҷre going to be adding different types of content and weвҖҷre going to end up undoing some of the changes weвҖҷve made. ItвҖҷs good because weвҖҷll be able to see immediate results. ItвҖҷs also good to start here because we can start to build the basic layout for the site. As we add more content types, weвҖҷll refactor this file and move logic around. Hugo makes that fairly painless, so weвҖҷll accept the cost and proceed. Please see the Hugo documentation on template rendering for all the details on determining which template to use. And, as the docs mention, if youвҖҷre building a single page application (SPA) web site, you can delete all of the other templates and work with just the default single page. ThatвҖҷs a refreshing amount of joy right there. Update the Template File $ vi themes/zafta/layouts/_default/single.html {{ .Title }}

{{ .Title }}

{{ .Content }} :wq $ Build the web site and verify the results. $ rm -rf public $ hugo --verbose INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 found taxonomies: map[string]string{"tag":"tags", "category":"categories"} WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html theme/404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 2 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 4 ms $ find public -type f -name '*.html' | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 94 Sep 29 22:40 public/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 125 Sep 29 22:40 public/post/first/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 22:40 public/post/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 128 Sep 29 22:40 public/post/second/index.html $ cat public/post/first/index.html first

first

my first post

$ cat public/post/second/index.html second

second

my second post

$ Notice that the posts now have content. You can go to localhost:1313/post/first to verify.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content:
Linking to Content The posts are on the home page. LetвҖҷs add a link from there to the post. Since this is the home page, weвҖҷll update its template. $ vi themes/zafta/layouts/index.html {{ range first 10 .Data.Pages }}

{{ .Title }}

{{ end }} Build the web site and verify the results. $ rm -rf public $ hugo --verbose INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 found taxonomies: map[string]string{"tag":"tags", "category":"categories"} WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html theme/404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 2 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 4 ms $ find public -type f -name '*.html' | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 149 Sep 29 22:44 public/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 125 Sep 29 22:44 public/post/first/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 22:44 public/post/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 128 Sep 29 22:44 public/post/second/index.html $ cat public/index.html

second

first

$
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Create a Post Listing We have the posts displaying on the home page and on their own page. We also have a file public/post/index.html that is empty. LetвҖҷs make it show a list of all posts (not just the first ten). We need to decide which template to update. This will be a listing, so it should be a list template. LetвҖҷs take a quick look and see which list templates are available. $ find themes/zafta -name list.html | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/_default/list.html As with the single post, we have to decide to update _default/list.html or create post/list.html. We still donвҖҷt have multiple content types, so letвҖҷs stay consistent and update the default list template.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content:
Creating Top Level Pages LetвҖҷs add an вҖңaboutвҖқ page and display it at the top level (as opposed to a sub-level like we did with posts). The default in Hugo is to use the directory structure of the content/ directory to guide the location of the generated html in the public/ directory. LetвҖҷs verify that by creating an вҖңaboutвҖқ page at the top level: $ vi content/about.md +++ title = "about" description = "about this site" date = "2014-09-27" slug = "about time" +++ ## about us i'm speechless :wq Generate the web site and verify the results. $ find public -name '*.html' | xargs ls -l -rw-rw-r-- 1 mdhender staff 334 Sep 27 15:08 public/about-time/index.html -rw-rw-r-- 1 mdhender staff 527 Sep 27 15:08 public/index.html -rw-rw-r-- 1 mdhender staff 358 Sep 27 15:08 public/post/first-post/index.html -rw-rw-r-- 1 mdhender staff 0 Sep 27 15:08 public/post/index.html -rw-rw-r-- 1 mdhender staff 342 Sep 27 15:08 public/post/second-post/index.html Notice that the page wasnвҖҷt created at the top level. It was created in a sub-directory named вҖҳabout-time/вҖҷ. That name came from our slug. Hugo will use the slug to name the generated content. ItвҖҷs a reasonable default, by the way, but we can learn a few things by fighting it for this file. One other thing. Take a look at the home page. $ cat public/index.html

creating a new theme

about

second

first

Notice that the вҖңaboutвҖқ link is listed with the posts? ThatвҖҷs not desirable, so letвҖҷs change that first. $ vi themes/zafta/layouts/index.html

posts

{{ range first 10 .Data.Pages }} {{ if eq .Type "post"}}

{{ .Title }}

{{ end }} {{ end }}

pages

{{ range .Data.Pages }} {{ if eq .Type "page" }}

{{ .Title }}

{{ end }} {{ end }} :wq Generate the web site and verify the results. The home page has two sections, posts and pages, and each section has the right set of headings and links in it. But, that about page still renders to about-time/index.html. $ find public -name '*.html' | xargs ls -l -rw-rw-r-- 1 mdhender staff 334 Sep 27 15:33 public/about-time/index.html -rw-rw-r-- 1 mdhender staff 645 Sep 27 15:33 public/index.html -rw-rw-r-- 1 mdhender staff 358 Sep 27 15:33 public/post/first-post/index.html -rw-rw-r-- 1 mdhender staff 0 Sep 27 15:33 public/post/index.html -rw-rw-r-- 1 mdhender staff 342 Sep 27 15:33 public/post/second-post/index.html Knowing that hugo is using the slug to generate the file name, the simplest solution is to change the slug. LetвҖҷs do it the hard way and change the permalink in the configuration file. $ vi config.toml [permalinks] page = "/:title/" about = "/:filename/" Generate the web site and verify that this didnвҖҷt work. Hugo lets вҖңslugвҖқ or вҖңURLвҖқ override the permalinks setting in the configuration file. Go ahead and comment out the slug in content/about.md, then generate the web site to get it to be created in the right place.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Sharing Templates If youвҖҷve been following along, you probably noticed that posts have titles in the browser and the home page doesnвҖҷt. ThatвҖҷs because we didnвҖҷt put the title in the home pageвҖҷs template (layouts/index.html). ThatвҖҷs an easy thing to do, but letвҖҷs look at a different option. We can put the common bits into a shared template thatвҖҷs stored in the themes/zafta/layouts/partials/ directory.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Create the Header and Footer Partials In Hugo, a partial is a sugar-coated template. Normally a template reference has a path specified. Partials are different. Hugo searches for them along a TODO defined search path. This makes it easier for end-users to override the themeвҖҷs presentation. $ vi themes/zafta/layouts/partials/header.html <!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <title>{{ .Title }}</title> </head> <body> :wq $ vi themes/zafta/layouts/partials/footer.html </body> </html> :wq
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content:
Update the Home Page Template to Use the Partials The most noticeable difference between a template call and a partials call is the lack of path: {{ template "theme/partials/header.html" . }} versus {{ partial "header.html" . }} Both pass in the context. LetвҖҷs change the home page template to use these new partials. $ vi themes/zafta/layouts/index.html {{ partial "header.html" . }}

posts

{{ range first 10 .Data.Pages }} {{ if eq .Type "post"}}

{{ .Title }}

{{ end }} {{ end }}

pages

{{ range .Data.Pages }} {{ if or (eq .Type "page") (eq .Type "about") }}

{{ .Type }} - {{ .Title }} - {{ .RelPermalink }}

{{ end }} {{ end }} {{ partial "footer.html" . }} :wq Generate the web site and verify the results. The title on the home page is now вҖңyour title hereвҖқ, which comes from the вҖңtitleвҖқ variable in the config.toml file.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content:
Update the Default Single Template to Use the Partials $ vi themes/zafta/layouts/_default/single.html {{ partial "header.html" . }}

{{ .Title }}

{{ .Content }} {{ partial "footer.html" . }} :wq Generate the web site and verify the results. The title on the posts and the about page should both reflect the value in the markdown file.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Add вҖңDate PublishedвҖқ to Posts ItвҖҷs common to have posts display the date that they were written or published, so letвҖҷs add that. The front matter of our posts has a variable named вҖңdate.вҖқ ItвҖҷs usually the date the content was created, but letвҖҷs pretend thatвҖҷs the value we want to display.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content:
Add вҖңDate PublishedвҖқ to the Template WeвҖҷll start by updating the template used to render the posts. The template code will look like: {{ .Date.Format "Mon, Jan 2, 2006" }} Posts use the default single template, so weвҖҷll change that file. $ vi themes/zafta/layouts/_default/single.html {{ partial "header.html" . }}

{{ .Title }}

{{ .Date.Format "Mon, Jan 2, 2006" }}

{{ .Content }} {{ partial "footer.html" . }} :wq Generate the web site and verify the results. The posts now have the date displayed in them. ThereвҖҷs a problem, though. The вҖңaboutвҖқ page also has the date displayed. As usual, there are a couple of ways to make the date display only on posts. We could do an вҖңifвҖқ statement like we did on the home page. Another way would be to create a separate template for posts. The вҖңifвҖқ solution works for sites that have just a couple of content types. It aligns with the principle of вҖңcode for today,вҖқ too. LetвҖҷs assume, though, that weвҖҷve made our site so complex that we feel we have to create a new template type. In Hugo-speak, weвҖҷre going to create a section template. LetвҖҷs restore the default single template before we forget. $ mkdir themes/zafta/layouts/post $ vi themes/zafta/layouts/_default/single.html {{ partial "header.html" . }}

{{ .Title }}

{{ .Content }} {{ partial "footer.html" . }} :wq Now weвҖҷll update the postвҖҷs version of the single template. If you remember HugoвҖҷs rules, the template engine will use this version over the default. $ vi themes/zafta/layouts/post/single.html {{ partial "header.html" . }}

{{ .Title }}

{{ .Date.Format "Mon, Jan 2, 2006" }}

{{ .Content }} {{ partial "footer.html" . }} :wq Note that we removed the date logic from the default template and put it in the post template. Generate the web site and verify the results. Posts have dates and the about page doesnвҖҷt.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: DonвҖҷt Repeat Yourself DRY is a good design goal and Hugo does a great job supporting it. Part of the art of a good template is knowing when to add a new template and when to update an existing one. While youвҖҷre figuring that out, accept that youвҖҷll be doing some refactoring. Hugo makes that easy and fast, so itвҖҷs okay to delay splitting up a template.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Introduction This tutorial will show you how to create a simple theme in Hugo. I assume that you are familiar with HTML, the bash command line, and that you are comfortable using Markdown to format content. IвҖҷll explain how Hugo uses templates and how you can organize your templates to create a theme. I wonвҖҷt cover using CSS to style your theme. WeвҖҷll start with creating a new site with a very basic template. Then weвҖҷll add in a few pages and posts. With small variations on that, you will be able to create many different types of web sites. In this tutorial, commands that you enter will start with the вҖң$вҖқ prompt. The output will follow. Lines that start with вҖң#вҖқ are comments that IвҖҷve added to explain a point. When I show updates to a file, the вҖң:wqвҖқ on the last line means to save the file. HereвҖҷs an example: ## this is a comment $ echo this is a command this is a command ## edit the file $vi foo.md +++ date = "2014-09-28" title = "creating a new theme" +++ bah and humbug :wq ## show it $ cat foo.md +++ date = "2014-09-28" title = "creating a new theme" +++ bah and humbug $
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Some Definitions There are a few concepts that you need to understand before creating a theme.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Skins Skins are the files responsible for the look and feel of your site. ItвҖҷs the CSS that controls colors and fonts, itвҖҷs the Javascript that determines actions and reactions. ItвҖҷs also the rules that Hugo uses to transform your content into the HTML that the site will serve to visitors. You have two ways to create a skin. The simplest way is to create it in the layouts/ directory. If you do, then you donвҖҷt have to worry about configuring Hugo to recognize it. The first place that Hugo will look for rules and files is in the layouts/ directory so it will always find the skin. Your second choice is to create it in a sub-directory of the themes/ directory. If you do, then you must always tell Hugo where to search for the skin. ItвҖҷs extra work, though, so why bother with it? The difference between creating a skin in layouts/ and creating it in themes/ is very subtle. A skin in layouts/ canвҖҷt be customized without updating the templates and static files that it is built from. A skin created in themes/, on the other hand, can be and that makes it easier for other people to use it. The rest of this tutorial will call a skin created in the themes/ directory a theme. Note that you can use this tutorial to create a skin in the layouts/ directory if you wish to. The main difference will be that you wonвҖҷt need to update the siteвҖҷs configuration file to use a theme.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: The Home Page The home page, or landing page, is the first page that many visitors to a site see. It is the index.html file in the root directory of the web site. Since Hugo writes files to the public/ directory, our home page is public/index.html.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Site Configuration File When Hugo runs, it looks for a configuration file that contains settings that override default values for the entire site. The file can use TOML, YAML, or JSON. I prefer to use TOML for my configuration files. If you prefer to use JSON or YAML, youвҖҷll need to translate my examples. YouвҖҷll also need to change the name of the file since Hugo uses the extension to determine how to process it. Hugo translates Markdown files into HTML. By default, Hugo expects to find Markdown files in your content/ directory and template files in your themes/ directory. It will create HTML files in your public/ directory. You can change this by specifying alternate locations in the configuration file.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Content Content is stored in text files that contain two sections. The first section is the вҖңfront matter,вҖқ which is the meta-information on the content. The second section contains Markdown that will be converted to HTML. Front Matter The front matter is information about the content. Like the configuration file, it can be written in TOML, YAML, or JSON. Unlike the configuration file, Hugo doesnвҖҷt use the fileвҖҷs extension to know the format. It looks for markers to signal the type. TOML is surrounded by вҖң+++вҖқ, YAML by вҖң---вҖқ, and JSON is enclosed in curly braces. I prefer to use TOML, so youвҖҷll need to translate my examples if you prefer YAML or JSON. The information in the front matter is passed into the template before the content is rendered into HTML. Markdown Content is written in Markdown which makes it easier to create the content. Hugo runs the content through a Markdown engine to create the HTML which will be written to the output file.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Template Files Hugo uses template files to render content into HTML. Template files are a bridge between the content and presentation. Rules in the template define what content is published, where itвҖҷs published to, and how it will rendered to the HTML file. The template guides the presentation by specifying the style to use. There are three types of templates: single, list, and partial. Each type takes a bit of content as input and transforms it based on the commands in the template. Hugo uses its knowledge of the content to find the template file used to render the content. If it canвҖҷt find a template that is an exact match for the content, it will shift up a level and search from there. It will continue to do so until it finds a matching template or runs out of templates to try. If it canвҖҷt find a template, it will use the default template for the site. Please note that you can use the front matter to influence HugoвҖҷs choice of templates. Single Template A single template is used to render a single piece of content. For example, an article or post would be a single piece of content and use a single template. List Template A list template renders a group of related content. That could be a summary of recent postings or all articles in a category. List templates can contain multiple groups. The homepage template is a special type of list template. Hugo assumes that the home page of your site will act as the portal for the rest of the content in the site. Partial Template A partial template is a template that can be included in other templates. Partial templates must be called using the вҖңpartialвҖқ template command. They are very handy for rolling up common behavior. For example, your site may have a banner that all pages use. Instead of copying the text of the banner into every single and list template, you could create a partial with the banner in it. That way if you decide to change the banner, you only have to change the partial template.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Create a New Site LetвҖҷs use Hugo to create a new web site. IвҖҷm a Mac user, so IвҖҷll create mine in my home directory, in the Sites folder. If youвҖҷre using Linux, you might have to create the folder first. The вҖңnew siteвҖқ command will create a skeleton of a site. It will give you the basic directory structure and a useable configuration file. $ hugo new site ~/Sites/zafta $ cd ~/Sites/zafta $ ls -l total 8 drwxr-xr-x 7 quoha staff 238 Sep 29 16:49 . drwxr-xr-x 3 quoha staff 102 Sep 29 16:49 .. drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 archetypes -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 82 Sep 29 16:49 config.toml drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 content drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 layouts drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 static $ Take a look in the content/ directory to confirm that it is empty. The other directories (archetypes/, layouts/, and static/) are used when customizing a theme. ThatвҖҷs a topic for a different tutorial, so please ignore them for now.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Generate the HTML For the New Site Running the hugo command with no options will read all the available content and generate the HTML files. It will also copy all static files (thatвҖҷs everything thatвҖҷs not content). Since we have an empty site, it wonвҖҷt do much, but it will do it very quickly. $ hugo --verbose INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [index.html _default/list.html _default/single.html] WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 0 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 2 ms $ The вҖң--verboseвҖқ flag gives extra information that will be helpful when we build the template. Every line of the output that starts with вҖңINFO:вҖқ or вҖңWARN:вҖқ is present because we used that flag. The lines that start with вҖңWARN:вҖқ are warning messages. WeвҖҷll go over them later. We can verify that the command worked by looking at the directory again. $ ls -l total 8 drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 archetypes -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 82 Sep 29 16:49 config.toml drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 content drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 layouts drwxr-xr-x 4 quoha staff 136 Sep 29 17:02 public drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 static $ See that new public/ directory? Hugo placed all generated content there. When youвҖҷre ready to publish your web site, thatвҖҷs the place to start. For now, though, letвҖҷs just confirm that we have what weвҖҷd expect from a site with no content. $ ls -l public total 16 -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 416 Sep 29 17:02 index.xml -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 262 Sep 29 17:02 sitemap.xml $ Hugo created two XML files, which is standard, but there are no HTML files.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Test the New Site Verify that you can run the built-in web server. It will dramatically shorten your development cycle if you do. Start it by running the вҖңserverвҖқ command. If it is successful, you will see output similar to the following: $ hugo server --verbose INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [index.html _default/list.html _default/single.html] WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 0 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 2 ms Serving pages from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public Web Server is available at http://localhost:1313 Press Ctrl+C to stop Connect to the listed URL (itвҖҷs on the line that starts with вҖңWeb ServerвҖқ). If everything is working correctly, you should get a page that shows the following: index.xml sitemap.xml ThatвҖҷs a listing of your public/ directory. Hugo didnвҖҷt create a home page because our site has no content. When thereвҖҷs no index.html file in a directory, the server lists the files in the directory, which is what you should see in your browser. LetвҖҷs go back and look at those warnings again. WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [index.html _default/list.html _default/single.html] WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html] That second warning is easier to explain. We havenвҖҷt created a template to be used to generate вҖңpage not found errors.вҖқ The 404 message is a topic for a separate tutorial. Now for the first warning. It is for the home page. You can tell because the first layout that it looked for was вҖңindex.html.вҖқ ThatвҖҷs only used by the home page. I like that the verbose flag causes Hugo to list the files that itвҖҷs searching for. For the home page, they are index.html, _default/list.html, and _default/single.html. There are some rules that weвҖҷll cover later that explain the names and paths. For now, just remember that Hugo couldnвҖҷt find a template for the home page and it told you so. At this point, youвҖҷve got a working installation and site that we can build upon. All thatвҖҷs left is to add some content and a theme to display it.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Create a New Theme Hugo doesnвҖҷt ship with a default theme. There are a few available (I counted a dozen when I first installed Hugo) and Hugo comes with a command to create new themes. WeвҖҷre going to create a new theme called вҖңzafta.вҖқ Since the goal of this tutorial is to show you how to fill out the files to pull in your content, the theme will not contain any CSS. In other words, ugly but functional. All themes have opinions on content and layout. For example, Zafta uses вҖңpostвҖқ over вҖңblogвҖқ. Strong opinions make for simpler templates but differing opinions make it tougher to use themes. When you build a theme, consider using the terms that other themes do.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Create a Skeleton Use the hugo вҖңnewвҖқ command to create the skeleton of a theme. This creates the directory structure and places empty files for you to fill out. $ hugo new theme zafta $ ls -l total 8 drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 archetypes -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 82 Sep 29 16:49 config.toml drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 content drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 layouts drwxr-xr-x 4 quoha staff 136 Sep 29 17:02 public drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 16:49 static drwxr-xr-x 3 quoha staff 102 Sep 29 17:31 themes $ find themes -type f | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 1081 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/LICENSE.md -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/archetypes/default.md -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/_default/list.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/_default/single.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/partials/footer.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/partials/header.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 93 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/theme.toml $ The skeleton includes templates (the files ending in .html), license file, a description of your theme (the theme.toml file), and an empty archetype. Please take a minute to fill out the theme.toml and LICENSE.md files. TheyвҖҷre optional, but if youвҖҷre going to be distributing your theme, it tells the world who to praise (or blame). ItвҖҷs also nice to declare the license so that people will know how they can use the theme. $ vi themes/zafta/theme.toml author = "michael d henderson" description = "a minimal working template" license = "MIT" name = "zafta" source_repo = "" tags = ["tags", "categories"] :wq ## also edit themes/zafta/LICENSE.md and change ## the bit that says "YOUR_NAME_HERE" Note that the the skeletonвҖҷs template files are empty. DonвҖҷt worry, weвҖҷll be changing that shortly. $ find themes/zafta -name '*.html' | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/_default/list.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/_default/single.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/partials/footer.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/partials/header.html $
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Update the Configuration File to Use the Theme Now that weвҖҷve got a theme to work with, itвҖҷs a good idea to add the theme name to the configuration file. This is optional, because you can always add вҖң-t zaftaвҖқ on all your commands. I like to put it the configuration file because I like shorter command lines. If you donвҖҷt put it in the configuration file or specify it on the command line, you wonвҖҷt use the template that youвҖҷre expecting to. Edit the file to add the theme, add a title for the site, and specify that all of our content will use the TOML format. $ vi config.toml theme = "zafta" baseurl = "" languageCode = "en-us" title = "zafta - totally refreshing" MetaDataFormat = "toml" :wq $
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Generate the Site Now that we have an empty theme, letвҖҷs generate the site again. $ hugo --verbose INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html theme/404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 0 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 2 ms $ Did you notice that the output is different? The warning message for the home page has disappeared and we have an additional information line saying that Hugo is syncing from the themeвҖҷs directory. LetвҖҷs check the public/ directory to see what HugoвҖҷs created. $ ls -l public total 16 drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 17:56 css -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:56 index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 407 Sep 29 17:56 index.xml drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 17:56 js -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 243 Sep 29 17:56 sitemap.xml $ Notice four things: Hugo created a home page. This is the file public/index.html. Hugo created a css/ directory. Hugo created a js/ directory. Hugo claimed that it created 0 pages. It created a file and copied over static files, but didnвҖҷt create any pages. ThatвҖҷs because it considers a вҖңpageвҖқ to be a file created directly from a content file. It doesnвҖҷt count things like the index.html files that it creates automatically. The Home Page Hugo supports many different types of templates. The home page is special because it gets its own type of template and its own template file. The file, layouts/index.html, is used to generate the HTML for the home page. The Hugo documentation says that this is the only required template, but that depends. HugoвҖҷs warning message shows that it looks for three different templates: WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [index.html _default/list.html _default/single.html] If it canвҖҷt find any of these, it completely skips creating the home page. We noticed that when we built the site without having a theme installed. When Hugo created our theme, it created an empty home page template. Now, when we build the site, Hugo finds the template and uses it to generate the HTML for the home page. Since the template file is empty, the HTML file is empty, too. If the template had any rules in it, then Hugo would have used them to generate the home page. $ find . -name index.html | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 20:21 ./public/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 17:31 ./themes/zafta/layouts/index.html $ The Magic of Static Hugo does two things when generating the site. It uses templates to transform content into HTML and it copies static files into the site. Unlike content, static files are not transformed. They are copied exactly as they are. Hugo assumes that your site will use both CSS and JavaScript, so it creates directories in your theme to hold them. Remember opinions? Well, HugoвҖҷs opinion is that youвҖҷll store your CSS in a directory named css/ and your JavaScript in a directory named js/. If you donвҖҷt like that, you can change the directory names in your theme directory or even delete them completely. HugoвҖҷs nice enough to offer its opinion, then behave nicely if you disagree. $ find themes/zafta -type d | xargs ls -ld drwxr-xr-x 7 quoha staff 238 Sep 29 17:38 themes/zafta drwxr-xr-x 3 quoha staff 102 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/archetypes drwxr-xr-x 5 quoha staff 170 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts drwxr-xr-x 4 quoha staff 136 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/_default drwxr-xr-x 4 quoha staff 136 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/layouts/partials drwxr-xr-x 4 quoha staff 136 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/static drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/static/css drwxr-xr-x 2 quoha staff 68 Sep 29 17:31 themes/zafta/static/js $
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: The Theme Development Cycle When youвҖҷre working on a theme, you will make changes in the themeвҖҷs directory, rebuild the site, and check your changes in the browser. Hugo makes this very easy: Purge the public/ directory. Run the built in web server in watch mode. Open your site in a browser. Update the theme. Glance at your browser window to see changes. Return to step 4. IвҖҷll throw in one more opinion: never work on a theme on a live site. Always work on a copy of your site. Make changes to your theme, test them, then copy them up to your site. For added safety, use a tool like Git to keep a revision history of your content and your theme. Believe me when I say that it is too easy to lose both your mind and your changes. Check the main Hugo site for information on using Git with Hugo.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Purge the public/ Directory When generating the site, Hugo will create new files and update existing ones in the public/ directory. It will not delete files that are no longer used. For example, files that were created in the wrong directory or with the wrong title will remain. If you leave them, you might get confused by them later. I recommend cleaning out your site prior to generating it. Note: If youвҖҷre building on an SSD, you should ignore this. Churning on a SSD can be costly.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: HugoвҖҷs Watch Option HugoвҖҷs вҖң--watchвҖқ option will monitor the content/ and your theme directories for changes and rebuild the site automatically.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Live Reload HugoвҖҷs built in web server supports live reload. As pages are saved on the server, the browser is told to refresh the page. Usually, this happens faster than you can say, вҖңWow, thatвҖҷs totally amazing.вҖқ
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Development Commands Use the following commands as the basis for your workflow. ## purge old files. hugo will recreate the public directory. ## $ rm -rf public ## ## run hugo in watch mode ## $ hugo server --watch --verbose HereвҖҷs sample output showing Hugo detecting a change to the template for the home page. Once generated, the web browser automatically reloaded the page. IвҖҷve said this before, itвҖҷs amazing. $ rm -rf public $ hugo server --watch --verbose INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html theme/404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 0 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 2 ms Watching for changes in /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/content Serving pages from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public Web Server is available at http://localhost:1313 Press Ctrl+C to stop INFO: 2014/09/29 File System Event: ["/Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/layouts/index.html": MODIFY|ATTRIB] Change detected, rebuilding site WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html theme/404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 0 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 1 ms
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Update the Home Page Template The home page is one of a few special pages that Hugo creates automatically. As mentioned earlier, it looks for one of three files in the themeвҖҷs layout/ directory: index.html _default/list.html _default/single.html We could update one of the default templates, but a good design decision is to update the most specific template available. ThatвҖҷs not a hard and fast rule (in fact, weвҖҷll break it a few times in this tutorial), but it is a good generalization.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content:
Make a Static Home Page Right now, that page is empty because we donвҖҷt have any content and we donвҖҷt have any logic in the template. LetвҖҷs change that by adding some text to the template. $ vi themes/zafta/layouts/index.html

hugo says hello!

:wq $ Build the web site and then verify the results. $ hugo --verbose INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html theme/404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 0 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 2 ms $ find public -type f -name '*.html' | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 78 Sep 29 21:26 public/index.html $ cat public/index.html

hugo says hello!

Live Reload Note: If youвҖҷre running the server with the --watch option, youвҖҷll see different content in the file: $ cat public/index.html

hugo says hello!

When you use --watch, the Live Reload script is added by Hugo. Look for live reload in the documentation to see what it does and how to disable it.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: Build a вҖңDynamicвҖқ Home Page вҖңDynamic home page?вҖқ HugoвҖҷs a static web site generator, so this seems an odd thing to say. I mean letвҖҷs have the home page automatically reflect the content in the site every time Hugo builds it. WeвҖҷll use iteration in the template to do that. Create New Posts Now that we have the home page generating static content, letвҖҷs add some content to the site. WeвҖҷll display these posts as a list on the home page and on their own page, too. Hugo has a command to generate a skeleton post, just like it does for sites and themes. $ hugo --verbose new post/first.md INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 attempting to create post/first.md of post INFO: 2014/09/29 curpath: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/archetypes/default.md ERROR: 2014/09/29 Unable to Cast <nil> to map[string]interface{} $ That wasnвҖҷt very nice, was it? The вҖңnewвҖқ command uses an archetype to create the post file. Hugo created an empty default archetype file, but that causes an error when thereвҖҷs a theme. For me, the workaround was to create an archetypes file specifically for the post type. $ vi themes/zafta/archetypes/post.md +++ Description = "" Tags = [] Categories = [] +++ :wq $ find themes/zafta/archetypes -type f | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 21:53 themes/zafta/archetypes/default.md -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 51 Sep 29 21:54 themes/zafta/archetypes/post.md $ hugo --verbose new post/first.md INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 attempting to create post/first.md of post INFO: 2014/09/29 curpath: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/archetypes/post.md INFO: 2014/09/29 creating /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/content/post/first.md /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/content/post/first.md created $ hugo --verbose new post/second.md INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 attempting to create post/second.md of post INFO: 2014/09/29 curpath: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/archetypes/post.md INFO: 2014/09/29 creating /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/content/post/second.md /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/content/post/second.md created $ ls -l content/post total 16 -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 104 Sep 29 21:54 first.md -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 105 Sep 29 21:57 second.md $ cat content/post/first.md +++ Categories = [] Description = "" Tags = [] date = "2014-09-29T21:54:53-05:00" title = "first" +++ my first post $ cat content/post/second.md +++ Categories = [] Description = "" Tags = [] date = "2014-09-29T21:57:09-05:00" title = "second" +++ my second post $ Build the web site and then verify the results. $ rm -rf public $ hugo --verbose INFO: 2014/09/29 Using config file: /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/config.toml INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/themes/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 syncing from /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/static/ to /Users/quoha/Sites/zafta/public/ INFO: 2014/09/29 found taxonomies: map[string]string{"category":"categories", "tag":"tags"} WARN: 2014/09/29 Unable to locate layout: [404.html theme/404.html] 0 draft content 0 future content 2 pages created 0 tags created 0 categories created in 4 ms $ The output says that it created 2 pages. Those are our new posts: $ find public -type f -name '*.html' | xargs ls -l -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 78 Sep 29 22:13 public/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 22:13 public/post/first/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 22:13 public/post/index.html -rw-r--r-- 1 quoha staff 0 Sep 29 22:13 public/post/second/index.html $ The new files are empty because because the templates used to generate the content are empty. The homepage doesnвҖҷt show the new content, either. We have to update the templates to add the posts.
title: [English] Creating a New Theme

content: List and Single Templates In Hugo, we have three major kinds of templates. ThereвҖҷs the home page template that we updated previously. It is used only by the home page. We also have вҖңsingleвҖқ templates which are used to generate output for a single content file. We also have вҖңlistвҖқ templates that are used to group multiple pieces of content before generating output.
